reasoning: The informal statement says the intersection of any collection of subspaces of V is a subspace of V. The Lean statement uses `u : ι → Submodule F V` (an indexed family over an arbitrary type ι, matching "any collection") and asserts `∃ U : Submodule F V, (⋂ i, (u i).carrier) = ↑U`, i.e., the set-theoretic intersection of the underlying carriers equals the carrier of some submodule U. Since a subset of V is a subspace iff it equals the carrier of some `Submodule F V`, this is exactly the statement "the intersection is a subspace." The ambient setting (F a field, V an AddCommGroup and F-module) correctly encodes "vector space over a field." The empty-ι edge case (where the intersection is `Set.univ`) is also handled correctly, since `⊤ : Submodule F V` has carrier V. Hence the Lean signature is logically equivalent to the informal claim.
